lessons.title: Your First Webpage
lessons.summary: Write HTML tags that browsers understand.

payload.title: Tags are HTML's building blocks
payload.body:
HTML uses tags like

to tell the browser what to show. An opening tag

and closing tag

wrap the content.

payload.prompt: What does <h1> create on a webpage?
payload.explanation: <h1> is the largest heading tag — it makes text big and bold.

payload.prompt: Close the heading tag correctly.
payload.explanation: Closing tags have a / before the tag name.

lessons.title: Paragraphs of Text
lessons.summary:
Use

to add paragraphs to your page.

payload.title: The paragraph tag
payload.body:
Wrap any block of text in

and

to make a paragraph. Browsers add a little space above and below automatically.

payload.prompt: Which tag is used for a paragraph of text?
payload.explanation: stands for paragraph.

payload.prompt: Wrap this sentence in a paragraph tag.
payload.explanation:
opens a paragraph,

closes it.

lessons.title: Clickable Links
lessons.summary: Use <a href> to link to another page.

payload.title: Anchor tags make links
payload.body: The <a> tag creates a link. The href attribute tells the browser where to go when clicked.

payload.prompt: Which attribute holds the URL in an <a> tag?
payload.explanation: href stands for Hypertext REFerence — the destination address.

payload.prompt: Complete the link tag attribute.
payload.explanation: href is the attribute that holds the link destination.

lessons.title: Adding Images
lessons.summary: Use to show pictures on your page.

payload.title: Images with
payload.body: is a self-closing tag — it doesn't need . The src attribute points to the image file, and alt describes it for screen readers.

payload.prompt: What does the src attribute in do?
payload.explanation: src is short for source — it tells the browser where to find the image.

payload.prompt: Add the correct attribute to show an image.
payload.explanation: src tells the browser where the image lives.

lessons.title: Building Lists
lessons.summary: Use <ul> and <li> to make bullet lists.

payload.title: Unordered lists
payload.body: <ul> creates a bullet list. Each item goes inside <li> (list item) tags. Use <ol> instead of <ul> for numbered lists.

payload.prompt: What does <li> stand for?
payload.explanation: li = list item. It goes inside <ul> or <ol>.

payload.prompt: Start a bullet list.
payload.explanation: <ul> creates an unordered (bullet) list.

lessons.title: Style with CSS
lessons.summary: Change colors and sizes with CSS.

payload.title: CSS makes pages beautiful
payload.body: CSS stands for Cascading Style Sheets. You can write CSS inside a <style> tag and target HTML elements by name. Use color to change text color.

payload.prompt: What does CSS control?
payload.explanation: CSS controls colors, fonts, sizes, spacing — everything visual.

payload.prompt: Pick the CSS property that changes text color.
payload.explanation: The color property sets the text color of an element.

payload.title: The humble <div>
payload.body: <div> is a generic box. Use it to group related content so you can style or position it together.

payload.prompt: What is <div> used for?
payload.explanation: <div> groups related elements together.

payload.prompt: Close the div block.
payload.explanation: Close with .

payload.title: <form> and <input>
payload.body: A <form> holds inputs. <input> boxes collect information. The type attribute controls what kind — text, number, email, password.

payload.prompt: Which attribute chooses the input kind?
payload.explanation: type="text", type="email", type="password", etc.

payload.prompt: Make a number input.
payload.explanation: type="number" shows a number input.

lessons.title: Tables
lessons.summary: Lay out data in rows and columns.

payload.title: <table>, <tr>, <td>
payload.body: A <table> holds rows (<tr>). Each row holds cells (<td> for data, <th> for header cells).

payload.prompt: Which tag marks one row in a table?
payload.explanation: <tr> = table row.

payload.prompt: Create a header cell.
payload.explanation: <th> makes a bold header cell.

payload.title: class and .selector
payload.body: Add class="card" to an element, then in CSS write .card { ... } to style every element with that class.

payload.prompt: How do you select a class in CSS?
payload.explanation: Class selectors start with a period.

payload.prompt: Add the card class to a div.
payload.explanation: class="card" tags it for CSS.

payload.title: <header>, <main>, <footer>
payload.body: Semantic tags describe the role of a section. Search engines and screen readers use them to understand your page better than plain <div>s.

payload.prompt: Which tag marks the main content area?
payload.explanation: <main> wraps the primary content.

payload.prompt: Pick the semantic tag for a page footer.
payload.explanation: <footer> describes the page footer.

lessons.title: Flexbox layout
lessons.summary: Arrange items in a row with one line of CSS.

payload.title: display: flex
payload.body: Flexbox lays out children in a row or column with spacing controls. Set display: flex on the parent — children line up automatically.

payload.prompt: Which property turns on flexbox?
payload.explanation: display: flex enables flex layout on a container.

payload.prompt: Make children line up horizontally.
payload.explanation: flex lays children out in a row by default.

payload.title: The <script> tag
payload.body: Put JavaScript inside <script> tags — usually just before </body>. The script can find elements and react to clicks.

payload.prompt: Which tag holds JavaScript inside an HTML page?
payload.explanation: <script> is the tag for JavaScript code.

payload.prompt: Open a script block.
payload.explanation: <script> ... </script> wraps JavaScript code.
